description: 'tiroe' is a point in time treated as having no duration. It is useful for an immediate boundary or change; 'shemu' (moment) is brief but broad enough to be lived through or remembered.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into high-front 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue for 'r', then round into stressed middle-height back 'o'. Unround and bring the tongue forward for the separate final middle-height 'e'.

semantic_domains.temporal: a temporal point considered without duration
